items.abstract_zh: 在大型语言模型 (LLM) 中，异常值是通过少数数量级极高的值来识别的，这严重影响了模型的准确性。研究人员提出了几种混合精度量化技术来管理这些激活异常值。这些方法采用了价值方面的异常值粒度，在平衡模型准确性和硬件效率方面面临着挑战。为了解决这个问题，我们利用了法学硕士的激活异常值通常聚集在特定渠道内的观察结果。因此，我们引入了 Oltron，一种全面的软件/硬件协同设计策略，用于具有层间/层内适应的 LLM 异常值感知量化。我们的方法包括三个关键创新：首先，一种新颖的量化算法，可以识别不同层和层内通道组之间异常值的最佳比例；其次，适应层间和层内分布的可重构架构；第三，基于图块的数据流优化器，可以为混合精度张量复杂地安排复杂的计算和内存访问。 Oltron 的性能优于最先进的异常值感知加速器 OliVe，实现了 1.9 倍的性能提升和 1.6 倍的能源效率，同时还提高了模型精度。

items.title: GDR-HGNN: A Heterogeneous Graph Neural Networks Accelerator Frontend with Graph Decoupling and Recoupling.
items.abstract: Heterogeneous Graph Neural Networks (HGNNs) have broadened the applicability of graph representation learning to heterogeneous graphs. However, the irregular memory access pattern of HGNNs leads to the buffer thrashing issue in HGNN accelerators.
items.abstract_zh: 异构图神经网络（HGNN）扩大了图表示学习对异构图的适用性。然而，HGNN 的不规则内存访问模式导致 HGNN 加速器中的缓冲区抖动问题。

items.title: EdGeo: A Physics-guided Generative AI Toolkit for Geophysical Monitoring on Edge Devices.
items.abstract: Full-waveform inversion (FWI) plays a vital role in geoscience to explore the subsurface. It utilizes the seismic wave to image the subsurface velocity map. As the machine learning (ML) technique evolves, the data-driven approaches using ML for FWI tasks have emerged, offering enhanced accuracy and reduced computational cost compared to traditional physics-based methods. However, a common challenge in geoscience --- the unprivileged data --- severely limits ML effectiveness. The issue becomes even worse during model pruning, a step essential in geoscience due to environmental complexities. To tackle this, we introduce the EdGeo toolkit, which employs a diffusion-based model guided by physics principles to generate high-fidelity velocity maps. The toolkit uses the acoustic wave equation to generate corresponding seismic waveform data, facilitating the fine-tuning of pruned ML models. Our results demonstrate significant improvements in SSIM scores and reduction in both MAE and MSE across various pruning ratios. Notably, the ML model fine-tuned using data generated by EdGeo yields superior quality of velocity maps, especially in representing unprivileged features, outperforming other existing methods.
items.abstract_zh: 全波形反演（FWI）在地球科学探索地下方面发挥着至关重要的作用。它利用地震波对地下速度图进行成像。随着机器学习 (ML) 技术的发展，使用 ML 执行 FWI 任务的数据驱动方法已经出现，与传统的基于物理的方法相比，它提供了更高的准确性并降低了计算成本。然而，地球科学中的一个常见挑战——非特权数据——严重限制了机器学习的有效性。由于环境的复杂性，模型修剪是地球科学中必不可少的一步，在模型修剪过程中，这个问题变得更加严重。为了解决这个问题，我们引入了 EdGeo 工具包，它采用物理原理指导的基于扩散的模型来生成高保真速度图。该工具包使用声波方程生成相应的地震波形数据，有助于对剪枝后的机器学习模型进行微调。我们的结果表明，在不同的剪枝率下，SSIM 分数显着提高，MAE 和 MSE 均降低。值得注意的是，使用 EdGeo 生成的数据进行微调的 ML 模型产生了卓越的速度图质量，特别是在表示非特权特征方面，优于其他现有方法。

items.title: Energy Efficient Dual Designs of FeFET-Based Analog In-Memory Computing with Inherent Shift-Add Capability.
items.abstract: In-memory computing (IMC) architecture emerges as a promising paradigm, improving the energy efficiency of multiply-and-accumulate (MAC) operations within deep neural networks (DNNs) by integrating the parallel computations within the memory arrays. Various high-precision analog IMC array designs have been developed based on both SRAM and emerging non-volatile memories (NVMs). These designs perform MAC operations of partial input and weight, with the corresponding partial products then fed into shift-add circuitry to produce the final MAC results. However, existing works often present intricate shift-add process for weight. The traditional digital shift-add process is limited in throughput due to time-multiplexing of ADCs, and advancing the shift-add process to the analog domain necessitates customized circuit implementations, resulting in compromises in energy and area efficiency. Furthermore, the joint optimization of the partial MAC operations and the weight shift-add process is rarely explored. In this paper, we propose novel, energy efficient dual designs of ferroelectric FET (FeFET) based high precision analog IMC featuring inherent shift-add capability. We introduce a FeFET based IMC paradigm that performs partial MAC in each column, and inherently integrates the shift-add process for 4-bit weights by leveraging FeFET's analog storage characteristics. This paradigm supports both 2's complement mode (2CM) and non-2's complement mode (N2CM) MAC, thereby offering flexible support for 4-/8-bit weight data in 2's complement format. Building upon this paradigm, we propose novel FeFET based dual designs, CurFe for the current mode and ChgFe for the charge mode, to accommodate the high precision analog domain IMC architecture. Evaluation results at circuit and system levels indicate that the circuit/system-level energy efficiency of the proposed FeFET-based analog IMC is 1.56×/1.37× higher when compared to the state-of-the-art analog IMC designs.
items.abstract_zh: 内存计算 (IMC) 架构作为一种有前途的范例出现，通过集成内存阵列内的并行计算来提高深度神经网络 (DNN) 内乘法累加 (MAC) 操作的能源效率。基于 SRAM 和新兴非易失性存储器 (NVM) 开发了各种高精度模拟 IMC 阵列设计。这些设计执行部分输入和权重的 MAC 运算，然后将相应的部分乘积输入移位加法电路以产生最终的 MAC 结果。然而，现有的工作经常呈现复杂的权重移位添加过程。由于 ADC 的时间复用，传统的数字移位加法过程的吞吐量受到限制，而将移位加法过程推进到模拟领域需要定制电路实现，从而导致能量和面积效率的妥协。此外，部分MAC操作和权重移位-添加过程的联合优化很少被探索。在本文中，我们提出了基于铁电 FET (FeFET) 的新型、节能的双设计高精度模拟 IMC，具有固有的移位加法功能。我们引入了基于 FeFET 的 IMC 范例，该范例在每列中执行部分 MAC，并通过利用 FeFET 的模拟存储特性固有地集成了 4 位权重的移位加法过程。该范例支持 2 的补码模式 (2CM) 和非 2 的补码模式 (N2CM) MAC，从而为 2 的补码格式的 4 位/8 位权重数据提供灵活的支持。在此范例的基础上，我们提出了基于 FeFET 的新型双设计，CurFe 用于电流模式，ChgFe 用于充电模式，以适应高精度模拟域 IMC 架构。电路和系统级的评估结果表明，与最先进的模拟 IMC 设计相比，所提出的基于 FeFET 的模拟 IMC 的电路/系统级能效高出 1.56 倍/1.37 倍。

items.title: EOS: An Energy-Oriented Attack Framework for Spiking Neural Networks.
items.abstract: Spiking neural networks (SNNs) are emerging as energy-efficient alternatives to traditional artificial neural networks (ANNs). Their event-driven information processing significantly reduces computational demands while maintaining competitive performance. However, as SNNs are increasingly deployed in edge devices, security concerns have emerged. While significant research efforts have been dedicated to addressing the security vulnerabilities stemming from malicious input, often referred to as adversarial examples, the security of SNN parameters remains relatively unexplored. This work introduces a novel attack methodology for SNNs known as Energy-Oriented SNN attack (EOS). EOS is designed to increase the energy consumption of SNNs through the malicious manipulation of binary bits within their memory systems (i.e., DRAM), where neuronal information is stored. The key insight of EOS lies in the observation that energy consumption in SNN implementations is intricately linked to spiking activity. The bit-flip operation, the well-known Row Hammer technique, is employed in EOS. It achieves this by identifying the most robust neurons in the SNN based on the spiking activity, particularly those related to the firing threshold, which is stored as binary bits in memory. EOS employs a combination of spiking activity analysis and a progressive search strategy to pinpoint the target neurons for bit-flip attacks. The primary objective is to incrementally increase the energy consumption of the SNN while ensuring that accuracy remains intact. With the implementation of EOS, successful attacks on SNNs can lead to an average of 43% energy increase with no drop in accuracy.
items.abstract_zh: 尖峰神经网络 (SNN) 正在成为传统人工神经网络 (ANN) 的节能替代品。它们的事件驱动信息处理显着降低了计算需求，同时保持了竞争性能。然而，随着 SNN 越来越多地部署在边缘设备中，安全问题也随之出现。尽管大量的研究工作致力于解决恶意输入（通常称为对抗性示例）引起的安全漏洞，但 SNN 参数的安全性仍然相对未经探索。这项工作引入了一种新颖的 SNN 攻击方法，称为面向能量的 SNN 攻击 (EOS)。 EOS 旨在通过恶意操纵存储神经元信息的存储系统（即 DRAM）内的二进制位来增加 SNN 的能耗。 EOS 的关键见解在于观察到 SNN 实现中的能源消耗与尖峰活动有着错综复杂的联系。 EOS 中采用了位翻转操作，即著名的 Row Hammer 技术。它通过根据尖峰活动识别 SNN 中最稳健的神经元来实现这一目标，特别是那些与激发阈值相关的神经元，这些神经元以二进制位形式存储在内存中。 EOS 采用尖峰活动分析和渐进式搜索策略相结合来精确定位位翻转攻击的目标神经元。主要目标是逐步增加 SNN 的能耗，同时确保准确性保持不变。随着 EOS 的实施，对 SNN 的成功攻击可以导致平均 43% 的能量增加，而准确率却没有下降。

items.title: FQP: A Fibonacci Quantization Processor with Multiplication-Free Computing and Topological-Order Routing.
items.abstract: With the continuous advancement of artificial intelligence, neural networks exhibit an escalating parameter size, demanding increased computational power and excessive memory access. Low bit-width quantization emerges as a viable solution to address this challenge. However, conventional low bit-width uniform quantization suffers from a mismatch with the weight and activation data distribution in neural networks, resulting in accuracy degradation.
items.abstract_zh: 随着人工智能的不断进步，神经网络的参数大小不断增加，需要更高的计算能力和过多的内存访问。低位宽量化成为应对这一挑战的可行解决方案。然而，传统的低位宽均匀量化与神经网络中的权重和激活数据分布不匹配，导致精度下降。

items.title: EGMA: Enhancing Data Reuse and Workload Balancing in Message Passing GNN Acceleration via Gram Matrix Optimization.
items.abstract: Graph Neural Networks (GNNs) have been widely used to handle intricate graph-related problems, in which complex vertex and edge operations are performed in the form of message passing between vertices. Such complex GNN operations are highly dependent on the graph structure and can no longer be characterized as sparse-dense or general matrix multiplications. Consequently, current matrix-based data reuse and workload balancing optimizations have limited applicability to Message Passing-based GNN acceleration. In this paper, we leverage the mathematical insights from Gram Matrix to simultaneously exploit data reuse and workload balancing opportunities for message passing-based GNN accelerations. Upon this insight, we further propose a novel accelerator, named EGMA, that can efficiently facilitate a wide range of GNN models with improved data reuse and workload balance. Consequently, EGMA can achieve performance speedup by 1.57×, 1.72×, and 1.43× and energy reduction by 38.19%, 34.02%, and 24.54% on average compared to Betty, FlowGNN, and ReGNN, respectively.
items.abstract_zh: 图神经网络（GNN）已被广泛用于处理复杂的图相关问题，其中复杂的顶点和边操作以顶点之间消息传递的形式执行。这种复杂的 GNN 运算高度依赖于图结构，不能再被描述为稀疏-密集或一般的矩阵乘法。因此，当前基于矩阵的数据重用和工作负载平衡优化对于基于消息传递的 GNN 加速的适用性有限。在本文中，我们利用 Gram Matrix 的数学见解，同时利用数据重用和工作负载平衡机会，实现基于消息传递的 GNN 加速。基于这一见解，我们进一步提出了一种名为 EGMA 的新型加速器，它可以有效地促进各种 GNN 模型，并改善数据重用和工作负载平衡。因此，与 Betty、FlowGNN 和 ReGNN 相比，EGMA 平均可以实现性能加速 1.57 倍、1.72 倍和 1.43 倍，能耗平均降低 38.19%、34.02% 和 24.54%。

items.title: Data-Efficient Conformalized Interval Prediction of Minimum Operating Voltage Capturing Process Variations.
items.abstract: Accurate minimum operating voltage (Vmin) prediction is a critical element in manufacturing tests. Conventional methods lack coverage guarantees in interval predictions. Conformal Prediction (CP), a distribution-free machine learning approach, excels in providing rigorous coverage guarantees for interval predictions. However, standard CP predictors may fail due to a lack of knowledge of process variations. We address this challenge by providing principled conformalized interval prediction in the presence of process variations with high data efficiency, where the data from a few additional chips is utilized for calibration. We demonstrate the superiority of the proposed method on industrial 16nm chip data.
items.abstract_zh: 准确的最小工作电压 (Vmin) 预测是制造测试中的关键要素。传统方法缺乏区间预测的覆盖保证。保形预测（CP）是一种无分布的机器学习方法，擅长为区间预测提供严格的覆盖保证。然而，由于缺乏对过程变化的了解，标准 CP 预测器可能会失败。我们通过在存在过程变化的情况下以高数据效率提供有原则的共形区间预测来应对这一挑战，其中来自一些附加芯片的数据用于校准。我们在工业 16nm 芯片数据上证明了所提出方法的优越性。

items.title: zeroTT: A Two-Step State Transition Avoidance Scheme for MLC STT-RAM.
items.abstract: Compared with conventional SRAM, Spin-Transfer Torque Random Access Memory(STT-RAM) is expected to play a crucial role in future memory technologies with the increasing demands for higher storage density and lower power consumption for modern embedded systems. Moreover, Multi-Level Cell (MLC) STT-RAM outperforms Single-Level Cell (SLC) STT-RAM since it has higher bit density. However, MLC STT-RAM suffers from write performance due to the two-step state transitions (TTs) in memory cells' soft domain. State-of-the-art approaches mitigate this issue by reducing TTs with efficient data coding. Unfortunately, none of the existing works can fully eliminate the TTs. In this work, zeroTT, an optimal (3, 4)-based expansion coding method that eliminates TTs for MLC STT-RAM. The design of ZeroTT considers space overhead and coding complexity, and our experimental results demonstrate that zeroTT can completely avoid TTs, leading to a more efficient MLC STT-RAM memory in terms of access latency, energy consumption, and device lifetime.
items.abstract_zh: 与传统的 SRAM 相比，随着现代嵌入式系统对更高存储密度和更低功耗的需求不断增加，自旋转移力矩随机存取存储器 (STT-RAM) 有望在未来的存储技术中发挥至关重要的作用。此外，多级单元 (MLC) STT-RAM 的性能优于单级单元 (SLC) STT-RAM，因为它具有更高的位密度。然而，由于存储单元软域中的两步状态转换 (TT)，MLC STT-RAM 的写入性能受到影响。最先进的方法通过有效的数据编码减少 TT 来缓解这个问题。不幸的是，现有的工作都不能完全消除 TT。在这项工作中，zeroTT 是一种基于 (3, 4) 的最佳扩展编码方法，可消除 MLC STT-RAM 的 TT。 ZeroTT 的设计考虑了空间开销和编码复杂性，我们的实验结果表明，zeroTT 可以完全避免 TT，从而在访问延迟、能耗和设备寿命方面实现更高效的 MLC STT-RAM 存储器。

items.title: Fused Sampling and Grouping with Search Space Reduction for Efficient Point Cloud Acceleration.
items.abstract: Recently, point-based deep neural networks (DNN) have demonstrated remarkable ability in analyzing point cloud data. However, challenges arise in sampling and grouping layers, particularly in terms of time and energy consumption due to the iterative access and computation of point cloud data for local feature extraction. In this paper, we introduce a Morton code-based data structure which stores point data with the shared upper bits together, enabling sequential access to the points within a specific voxel. We also propose a fused sampling and grouping approach with a reduced search space, which reuses the point data and the calculated distances for the farthest voxel and its neighbors. Additionally, a dedicated hardware architecture is introduced to maximize the efficiency of the proposed optimization technique. Experimental results show that our approach effectively reduces the number of distance calculations and data accesses with negligible accuracy loss, without requiring retraining of the network model.
items.abstract_zh: 最近，基于点的深度神经网络（DNN）在分析点云数据方面表现出了卓越的能力。然而，在采样和分组层中出现了挑战，特别是由于用于局部特征提取的点云数据的迭代访问和计算而导致的时间和能耗方面的挑战。在本文中，我们介绍了一种基于莫顿代码的数据结构，它将点数据与共享的高位存储在一起，从而能够顺序访问特定体素内的点。我们还提出了一种减少搜索空间的融合采样和分组方法，该方法重用点数据和最远体素及其邻居的计算距离。此外，还引入了专用硬件架构，以最大限度地提高所提出的优化技术的效率。实验结果表明，我们的方法有效地减少了距离计算和数据访问的次数，并且精度损失可以忽略不计，并且不需要重新训练网络模型。

items.title: Efficient Code Generation for Data-Intensive Simulink Models via Redundancy Elimination.
items.abstract: Simulink has emerged as the fundamental infrastructure that supports modeling, simulation, verification, and code generation for embedded software development. To improve the performance of the code generated from Simulink models, state-of-the-art code generators employ various optimization techniques, such as expression folding, variable reuse, and parallelism. However, they overlook the presence of redundant calculations within data-intensive models widely used to perform substantial data processing in embedded scenarios, which can significantly undermine the efficiency and performance of the generated code.
items.abstract_zh: Simulink 已成为支持嵌入式软件开发的建模、仿真、验证和代码生成的基础设施。为了提高 Simulink 模型生成的代码的性能，最先进的代码生成器采用了各种优化技术，例如表达式折叠、变量重用和并行性。然而，他们忽视了广泛用于在嵌入式场景中执行大量数据处理的数据密集型模型中存在的冗余计算，这可能会显着降低生成代码的效率和性能。

items.title: CDA-GNN: A Chain-driven Accelerator for Efficient Asynchronous Graph Neural Network.
items.abstract: Asynchronous Graph Neural Network (AGNN) has attracted much research attention because it enables faster convergence speed than the synchronous GNN. However, existing software/hardware solutions suffer from redundant computation overhead and excessive off-chip communications for AGNN due to irregular state propagations along the dependency chains between vertices. This paper proposes a chain-driven asynchronous accelerator, CDA-GNN, for efficient AGNN inference. Specifically, CDA-GNN proposes a chain-driven asynchronous execution approach into novel accelerator design to regularize the vertex state propagations for fewer redundant computations and off-chip communications and also designs a chain-aware data caching method to improve data locality for AGNN. We have implemented and evaluated CDA-GNN on a Xilinx Alveo U280 FPGA card. Compared with the cutting-edge software solutions (i.e., Dorylus and AMP) and hardware solutions (i.e., BlockGNN and FlowGNN), CDA-GNN improves the performance of AGNN inference by an average of 1,173x, 182.4x, 10.2x, and 7.9x and saves energy by 2,241x, 242.2x, 12.4x, and 8.9x, respectively.
items.abstract_zh: 异步图神经网络（AGNN）因其比同步 GNN 更快的收敛速度而引起了广泛的研究关注。然而，由于沿着顶点之间的依赖链的不规则状态传播，现有的软件/硬件解决方案面临冗余计算开销和过多的 AGNN 片外通信。本文提出了一种链驱动的异步加速器 CDA-GNN，用于高效的 AGNN 推理。具体来说，CDA-GNN 在新颖的加速器设计中提出了一种链驱动的异步执行方法，以规范顶点状态传播，以减少冗余计算和片外通信，并设计一种链感知数据缓存方法来提高 AGNN 的数据局部性。我们在 Xilinx Alveo U280 FPGA 卡上实现并评估了 CDA-GNN。与前沿的软件解决方案（Dorylus、AMP）和硬件解决方案（BlockGNN、FlowGNN）相比，CDA-GNN 将 AGNN 推理性能平均提升 1,173 倍、182.4 倍、10.2 倍、7.9 倍，节能 2,241 倍、242.2 倍、12.4 倍和分别为 8.9 倍。

items.title: RTGA: A Redundancy-free Accelerator for High-Performance Temporal Graph Neural Network Inference.
items.abstract: Temporal Graph Neural Network (TGNN) has attracted much research attention because it can capture the dynamic nature of complex networks. However, existing solutions suffer from redundant computation overhead and excessive off-chip communications for TGNN inference because they often rely on redundant graph sampling and repeatedly fetching the features and vertex memory. This paper proposes a redundancy-free accelerator, RTGA, for high-performance TGNN inference. Specifically, RTGA proposes a redundancy-aware execution approach with temporal tree into a novel accelerator design to effectively eliminate unnecessary data processing for fewer redundant computations and off-chip communications and also designs a temporal-aware data caching method to improve data locality for TGNN. We have implemented and evaluated RTGA on a Xilinx Alveo U280 FPGA card. Compared with cutting-edge software solutions (i.e., TGN and TGL) and hardware solutions (i.e., BlockGNN and FlowGNN), RTGA improves the performance of TGNN inference by an average of 473.2x, 87.4x, 8.2x, and 6.9x and saves energy by 542.8x, 102.2x, 9.4x, and 8.3x, respectively.
items.abstract_zh: 时态图神经网络（TGNN）因其能够捕获复杂网络的动态性质而引起了广泛的研究关注。然而，现有的解决方案面临着冗余计算开销和用于 TGNN 推理的过多片外通信，因为它们通常依赖于冗余图采样并重复获取特征和顶点内存。本文提出了一种用于高性能 TGNN 推理的无冗余加速器 RTGA。具体来说，RTGA 在新颖的加速器设计中提出了一种带有时间树的冗余感知执行方法，以有效消除不必要的数据处理，以减少冗余计算和片外通信，并设计了一种时间感知数据缓存方法来提高 TGNN 的数据局部性。我们在 Xilinx Alveo U280 FPGA 卡上实现并评估了 RTGA。与前沿的软件解决方案（TGN、TGL）和硬件解决方案（BlockGNN、FlowGNN）相比，RTGA 的 TGNN 推理性能平均提升了 473.2 倍、87.4 倍、8.2 倍和 6.9 倍，节能分别为 542.8 倍、102.2 倍、9.4 倍和 8.3 倍。

items.title: SPFuzz: Stateful Path based Parallel Fuzzing for Protocols in Autonomous Vehicles.
items.abstract: Protocols in autonomous vehicles are essential for efficient in-vehicle network communication. To ensure their security, many research efforts have been paid to the fuzz testing of their implementations. However, those fuzzing optimizations often struggle to manage the protocols' complex state, resulting in low efficiency in branch covering and vulnerability detection.
items.abstract_zh: 自动驾驶汽车中的协议对于高效的车内网络通信至关重要。为了确保其安全性，许多研究工作都花在了其实现的模糊测试上。然而，这些模糊优化往往难以管理协议的复杂状态，导致分支覆盖和漏洞检测效率低下。

items.title: EDGE-LLM: Enabling Efficient Large Language Model Adaptation on Edge Devices via Unified Compression and Adaptive Layer Voting.
items.abstract: Efficient adaption of large language models (LLMs) on edge devices is essential for applications requiring continuous and privacy-preserving adaptation and inference. However, existing tuning techniques fall short because of the high computation and memory overhead. To this end, we introduce a computation- and memory-efficient LLM tuning framework, called Edge-LLM, to facilitate affordable and effective LLM adaptation on edge devices. Specifically, Edge-LLM features three core components: (1) a layer-wise unified compression (LUC) technique to reduce the computation overhead by generating layer-wise pruning sparsity and quantization bit-width policies, (2) an adaptive layer tuning and voting scheme to reduce the memory overhead by reducing the backpropagation depth, and (3) a complementary hardware scheduling strategy to handle the irregular computation patterns introduced by LUC and adaptive layer tuning, thereby achieving improved real hardware efficiency. Extensive experiments demonstrate that Edge-LLM achieves on-device adaptation with comparable task accuracy as vanilla tuning methods with a 2.92× speed up and a 4× reduction in memory overhead. Our code is available at https://github.com/GATECH-EIC/Edge-LLM
items.abstract_zh: 对于需要连续且保护隐私的适应和推理的应用程序来说，边缘设备上大语言模型 (LLM) 的高效适应至关重要。然而，由于计算和内存开销较高，现有的调优技术存在不足。为此，我们引入了一种计算和内存高效的 LLM 调优框架，称为 Edge-LLM，以促进边缘设备上经济且有效的 LLM 适应。具体来说，Edge-LLM具有三个核心组件：（1）分层统一压缩（LUC）技术，通过生成分层修剪稀疏性和量化位宽策略来减少计算开销；（2）自适应层调整和投票方案，通过减少反向传播深度来减少内存开销；（3）补充硬件调度策略，处理LUC和自适应层调整引入的不规则计算模式，从而提高实际硬件效率。大量实验表明，Edge-LLM 实现了设备上自适应，其任务精度与普通调整方法相当，速度提高了 2.92 倍，内存开销降低了 4 倍。我们的代码位于 https://github.com/GATECH-EIC/Edge-LLM

items.title: CAP: A General Purpose Computation-in-memory with Content Addressable Processing Paradigm.
items.abstract: Demands for efficient computing under memory wall have led to computation-in-memory (CIM) accelerators that leverage memory structure to perform in-situ computing. The content addressable memory (CAM) processing is a CIM paradigm that accomplishes general purpose functions, via sequences of search and update operations on CAM. However, the conventional CAM-based CIM is customized for vector-vector operation only and requires long search-update iterations for computing.
items.abstract_zh: 对内存墙下高效计算的需求催生了利用内存结构执行原位计算的内存计算（CIM）加速器。内容可寻址存储器 (CAM) 处理是一种 CIM 范例，它通过 CAM 上的搜索和更新操作序列来完成通用功能。然而，传统的基于CAM的CIM仅针对向量-向量运算而定制，并且需要长时间的搜索更新迭代来进行计算。

items.title: Net Resource Allocation: A Desirable Initial Routing Step.
items.abstract: In modern IC design, routing significantly impacts chip performance, power, area, and design iteration count. Critical challenges in routing include generating a rectilinear Steiner minimum tree (RSMT) for each net and handling routing resources among nets. Due to limited resources and net order, congestion is inevitable in VLSI circuit routing. Most competitive routers address congestion after routing without prior net guidance, leading to difficulty in managing resources among nets. We suggest introducing a net resource allocation step to tackle routing and congestion as a potentially desirable initial routing stage. Firstly, we introduce the net region probability density (NRPD) concept to achieve suitable net resource allocation. Using a prior NRPD, we model the resource allocation problem as linear programming (LP). We solve the LP problem and obtain a posterior NRPD for each net on each grid. Based on the posterior NRPD and congestion map, we introduce a cost scheme to guide net routing. This cost scheme supports a weighted RSMT construction technique for better topological solutions. We propose an iterative method for global routing and track assignment, improving detailed routing quality and optimizing design rule violations. Experimental results show the effectiveness of net resource allocation and demonstrate the superior performance of our router over OpenROAD's router across multiple metrics.
items.abstract_zh: 在现代 IC 设计中，布线显着影响芯片性能、功耗、面积和设计迭代次数。路由中的关键挑战包括为每个网络生成直线斯坦纳最小树（RSMT）以及处理网络之间的路由资源。由于资源和网络秩序的限制，VLSI电路布线中的拥塞是不可避免的。大多数竞争性路由器在没有事先网络指导的情况下在路由后解决拥塞问题，从而导致管理网络间资源的困难。我们建议引入网络资源分配步骤来解决路由和拥塞问​​题，作为可能理想的初始路由阶段。首先，我们引入净区域概率密度（NRPD）概念来实现适当的净资源分配。使用先前的 NRPD，我们将资源分配问题建模为线性规划 (LP)。我们解决 LP 问题并获得每个网格上每个网络的后验 NRPD。基于后验 NRPD 和拥塞图，我们引入了一种成本方案来指导网络路由。该成本方案支持加权 RSMT 构造技术，以获得更好的拓扑解决方案。我们提出了一种用于全局布线和轨道分配的迭代方法，提高了详细布线质量并优化了设计规则违规。实验结果显示了网络资源分配的有效性，并证明了我们的路由器在多个指标上优于 OpenROAD 路由器的性能。

items.title: Safe Controller Synthesis for Nonlinear Systems via Reinforcement Learning and PAC Approximation.
items.abstract: Controller synthesis for nonlinear systems is an important research issue. Deep Neural Network (DNN) control policies obtained through reinforcement learning (RL), though exhibiting good performance in simulations, cannot be applied to safety-critical systems for lack of formal guarantee. To address this, this paper considers fully utilizing the advantages of RL for complex control tasks to obtain a well-performing DNN controller. Then, using PAC (Probably Approximately Correct) techniques, a polynomial surrogate controller with probabilistically controllable approximation error is obtained. Finally, the safety of the control system under the designed polynomial controller is verified using barrier certificate generation. Experiments demonstrate the effectiveness of our method in generating controllers with safety guarantees for systems with high dimensions and degrees.
items.abstract_zh: 非线性系统的控制器综合是一个重要的研究问题。通过强化学习（RL）获得的深度神经网络（DNN）控制策略虽然在模拟中表现出良好的性能，但由于缺乏形式保证而无法应用于安全关键系统。为了解决这个问题，本文考虑充分利用强化学习在复杂控制任务中的优势来获得性能良好的 DNN 控制器。然后，利用PAC（Probically approximate Correct）技术，获得了近似误差概率可控的多项式代理控制器。最后，使用屏障证书生成来验证所设计的多项式控制器下控制系统的安全性。实验证明了我们的方法在生成具有高维度和高阶系统安全保证的控制器方面的有效性。

items.title: Hyb-Learn: A Framework for On-Device Self-Supervised Continual Learning with Hybrid RRAM/SRAM Memory.
items.abstract: While RRAM crossbar-based In-Memory Computing (IMC) has proven highly effective in accelerating Deep Neural Networks (DNNs) inference, RRAM-based on-device training is less explored due to its high energy consumption of weight re-programming and cells' low endurance problem. Besides, emerging trends indicate a need for on-device continual learning which sequentially acquires knowledge from multiple tasks to enhance user's experiences and eliminate data privacy concerns. However, learning on each new task leads to forgetting prior learned knowledge on prior tasks, which is known as catastrophic forgetting. To address these challenges, we are the first to propose a novel training framework, Hyb-Learn, for enabling on-device continual learning with a hybrid RRAM/SRAM IMC architecture design. Specifically, when training each new arriving task, our approach first partitions the model into two groups based on the proposed task-correlated PE-wise correlation to freeze or re-training, and correspondingly mapping to RRAM and SRAM, respectively. In practice, the RRAM stores frozen weights with strong task correlation to prior tasks to eliminate the high cost of weight reprogramming issue of RRAM, while the SRAM stores the remaining weights that will be updated. Furthermore, to maximize the freezing ratio for improving training efficiency while maintaining accuracy and mitigating catastrophic forgetting, we incorporate self-supervised learning algorithms that are initialized from a pre-trained model for training each new task.
items.abstract_zh: 虽然基于 RRAM 交叉开关的内存计算 (IMC) 已被证明在加速深度神经网络 (DNN) 推理方面非常有效，但由于权重重新编程的高能耗和单元的低耐用性问题，基于 RRAM 的设备上训练较少被探索。此外，新兴趋势表明需要设备上持续学习，从多个任务中顺序获取知识，以增强用户体验并消除数据隐私问题。然而，学习每个新任务会导致忘记先前学习的先前任务知识，这被称为灾难性遗忘。为了应对这些挑战，我们率先提出了一种新颖的训练框架 Hyb-Learn，用于通过混合 RRAM/SRAM IMC 架构设计实现设备上持续学习。具体来说，在训练每个新到达的任务时，我们的方法首先根据所提出的任务相关的 PE-wise 相关性将模型划分为两组，以冻结或重新训练，并分别相应地映射到 RRAM 和 SRAM。实际上，RRAM 存储与先前任务具有强任务相关性的冻结权重，以消除 RRAM 权重重新编程问题的高成本问题，而 SRAM 存储将更新的剩余权重。此外，为了最大化冻结率以提高训练效率，同时保持准确性并减轻灾难性遗忘，我们采用了自监督学习算法，这些算法是从预训练模型初始化的，用于训练每个新任务。

items.title: ThermalScope: A Practical Interrupt Side Channel Attack Based on Thermal Event Interrupts.
items.abstract: While interrupts play a critical role in modern OSes, they have been exploited as a wide range of side channel attacks to break system confidentiality, such as keystroke interrupts, graphic interrupts and network interrupts. In this paper, we propose ThermalScope, a new side channel that exploits thermal event interrupts, which is adaptable for both native and browser scenarios and incorporates two heat amplifying techniques. The thermal event interrupts are activated only when the CPU package temperature reaches a fixed threshold that is determined by manufacturers. Our key observation is that workloads running on CPUs inevitably generates their distinct heat, which can be correlated with the thermal event interrupts. To demonstrate the viability of ThermalScope, we conduct a comprehensive evaluation on multiple Ubuntu OSes with different Intel-based CPUs. First, we show that the activation of thermal event interrupts correlates with the level of CPU temperature. We then apply ThermalScope to mount different side channel attacks, i.e., building covert channels with a transmission rate of 0.1 b/s, fingerprinting DNN model architectures with an accuracy of over 90% and breaking KASLR within 8.2 hours.
items.abstract_zh: 虽然中断在现代操作系统中发挥着关键作用，但它们已被利用作为各种侧通道攻击来破坏系统机密性，例如击键中断、图形中断和网络中断。在本文中，我们提出了 ThermalScope，这是一种利用热事件中断的新侧通道，它适用于本机和浏览器场景，并结合了两种热放大技术。仅当 CPU 封装温度达到制造商确定的固定阈值时，热事件中断才会激活。我们的主要观察结果是，在 CPU 上运行的工作负载不可避免地会产生独特的热量，这可能与热事件中断相关。为了证明 ThermalScope 的可行性，我们对具有不同基于 Intel 的 CPU 的多个 Ubuntu 操作系统进行了全面评估。首先，我们证明热事件中断的激活与 CPU 温度水平相关。然后，我们应用ThermalScope进行不同的侧通道攻击，即构建传输速率为0.1 b/s的隐蔽通道，以超过90%的准确度对DNN模型架构进行指纹识别，并在8.2小时内突破KASLR。

items.title: FNM-Trans: Efficient FPGA-based Transformer Architecture with Full N: M Sparsity.
Laser Shield: a Physical Defense with Polarizer against Laser Attacks on Autonomous Driving Systems.
items.abstract: Autonomous driving systems (ADS) are boosted with deep neural networks (DNN) to perceive environments, while their security is doubted by DNN's vulnerability to adversarial attacks. Among them, a diversity of laser attacks emerges to be a new threat due to its minimal requirements and high attack success rate in the physical world. Nevertheless, current defense methods exhibit either a low defense success rate or a high computation cost against laser attacks. To fill this gap, we propose Laser Shield which leverages a polarizer along with a min-energy rotation mechanism to eliminate adversarial lasers from ADS scenes. We also provide a physical world dataset, LAPA, to evaluate its performance. Through exhaustive experiments with three baselines, four metrics, and three settings, Laser Shield is proved to surpass SOTA performance.
items.abstract_zh: 自动驾驶系统 (ADS) 借助深度神经网络 (DNN) 来感知环境，但由于 DNN 容易受到对抗性攻击，其安全性受到质疑。其中，多样化的激光攻击因其在物理世界中的最低要求和较高的攻击成功率而成为一种新的威胁。然而，当前的防御方法对于激光攻击要么防御成功率低，要么计算成本高。为了填补这一空白，我们提出了激光防护罩，它利用偏振器和最小能量旋转机制来消除 ADS 场景中的敌对激光。我们还提供物理世界数据集 LAPA 来评估其性能。通过三个基线、四个指标和三个设置的详尽实验，Laser Shield 被证明超越了 SOTA 性能。

items.title: Hardware-Aware Neural Dropout Search for Reliable Uncertainty Prediction on FPGA.
items.abstract: The increasing deployment of artificial intelligence (AI) for critical decision-making amplifies the necessity for trustworthy AI, where uncertainty estimation plays a pivotal role in ensuring trustworthiness. Dropout-based Bayesian Neural Networks (BayesNNs) are prominent in this field, offering reliable uncertainty estimates. Despite their effectiveness, existing dropout-based BayesNNs typically employ a uniform dropout design across different layers, leading to suboptimal performance. Moreover, as diverse applications require tailored dropout strategies for optimal performance, manually optimizing dropout configurations for various applications is both error-prone and labor-intensive. To address these challenges, this paper proposes a novel neural dropout search framework that automatically optimizes both the dropout-based BayesNNs and their hardware implementations on FPGA. We leverage one-shot supernet training with an evolutionary algorithm for efficient dropout optimization. A layer-wise dropout search space is introduced to enable the automatic design of dropout-based BayesNNs with heterogeneous dropout configurations. Extensive experiments demonstrate that our proposed framework can effectively find design configurations on the Pareto frontier. Compared to manually-designed dropout-based BayesNNs on GPU, our search approach produces FPGA designs that can achieve up to 33X higher energy efficiency. Compared to state-of-the-art FPGA designs of BayesNN, the solutions from our approach can achieve higher algorithmic performance and energy efficiency.
items.abstract_zh: 人工智能（AI）越来越多地用于关键决策，增强了对可信人工智能的需求，其中不确定性估计在确保可信性方面发挥着关键作用。基于 Dropout 的贝叶斯神经网络 (BayesNN) 在该领域很突出，可以提供可靠的不确定性估计。尽管它们很有效，但现有的基于 dropout 的贝叶斯神经网络通常在不同层上采用统一的 dropout 设计，从而导致性能不佳。此外，由于不同的应用需要定制的 dropout 策略才能获得最佳性能，因此手动优化各种应用的 dropout 配置既容易出错，又费力。为了应对这些挑战，本文提出了一种新颖的神经 dropout 搜索框架，该框架可以自动优化基于 dropout 的 BayesNN 及其在 FPGA 上的硬件实现。我们利用一次性超网训练和进化算法来实现高效的 dropout 优化。引入逐层 dropout 搜索空间，以实现具有异构 dropout 配置的基于 dropout 的 BayesNN 的自动设计。大量的实验表明，我们提出的框架可以有效地找到帕累托前沿上的设计配置。与 GPU 上手动设计的基于 dropout 的 BayesNN 相比，我们的搜索方法产生的 FPGA 设计可以实现高达 33 倍的能源效率。与 BayesNN 最先进的 FPGA 设计相比，我们的方法的解决方案可以实现更高的算法性能和能源效率。

items.title: EmMark: Robust Watermarks for IP Protection of Embedded Quantized Large Language Models.
items.abstract: This paper introduces EmMark, a novel watermarking framework for protecting intellectual property (IP) of embedded large language models deployed on resource-constrained edge devices. To address the IP theft risks posed by malicious end-users, EmMark enables proprietors to authenticate ownership by querying the watermarked model weights and matching the inserted signatures. EmMark's novelty lies in its strategic watermark weight parameters selection, ensuring robustness and maintaining model quality. Extensive proof-of-concept evaluations of models from OPT and LLaMA-2 families demonstrate EmMark's fidelity, achieving 100% success in watermark extraction with model performance preservation. EmMark also showcased its resilience against watermark removal and forging attacks.
items.abstract_zh: 本文介绍了 EmMark，这是一种新颖的水印框架，用于保护部署在资源受限的边缘设备上的嵌入式大型语言模型的知识产权 (IP)。为了解决恶意最终用户带来的 IP 盗窃风险，EmMark 使所有者能够通过查询带水印的模型权重并匹配插入的签名来验证所有权。 EmMark的新颖之处在于其战略性水印权重参数选择，确保鲁棒性并保持模型质量。对 OPT 和 LLaMA-2 系列模型的广泛概念验证评估证明了 EmMark 的保真度，在保留模型性能的情况下在水印提取方面取得了 100% 的成功。 EmMark 还展示了其抵御水印去除和伪造攻击的能力。

items.title: AdderNet 2.0: Optimal FPGA Acceleration of AdderNet with Activation-Oriented Quantization and Fused Bias Removal based Memory Optimization.
items.abstract: Convolutional neural networks (CNNs) are computationally demanding due to expensive Multiply-ACcumulate (MAC) operations. Emerging neural network models, such as AdderNet, exploit efficient arithmetic alternatives like sum-of-absolute-difference (SAD) operations to replace the costly MAC operations, while still achieving competitive model accuracy as the CNN counterparts. Nevertheless, existing AdderNet accelerators still face critical implementation challenges to achieve maximal hardware and energy efficiency. This paper presents AdderNet 2.0, an algorithm-hardware co-design framework featuring a novel Activation-Oriented Quantization (AOQ) strategy, a Fused Bias Removal (FBR) scheme for on-chip feature memory bitwidth reduction, and optimal PE designs to improve the overall resource utilization towards optimal AdderNet accelerator designs. Multiple AdderNet 2.0 accelerator design variants were implemented on Xilinx KV-260 FPGA. Experimental results show that the INT6 AdderNet 2.0 accelerators achieve significant hardware resource and energy savings when compared to prior CNN and AdderNet designs.
items.abstract_zh: 由于昂贵的乘法累加 (MAC) 操作，卷积神经网络 (CNN) 的计算要求很高。 AdderNet 等新兴神经网络模型利用绝对差和 (SAD) 运算等高效算术替代方案来取代成本高昂的 MAC 运算，同时仍能实现与 CNN 模型相当的竞争模型精度。尽管如此，现有的 AdderNet 加速器仍然面临着实现最大硬件和能源效率的关键实施挑战。本文介绍了 AdderNet 2.0，这是一种算法-硬件协同设计框架，具有新颖的面向激活量化 (AOQ) 策略、用于减少片上特征存储器位宽的融合偏差消除 (FBR) 方案以及用于提高整体资源利用率的最佳 PE 设计，以实现最佳 AdderNet 加速器设计。在 Xilinx KV-260 FPGA 上实现了多个 AdderNet 2.0 加速器设计变体。实验结果表明，与之前的 CNN 和 AdderNet 设计相比，INT6 AdderNet 2.0 加速器可显着节省硬件资源和能源。

items.title: G-PASTA: GPU-Accelerated Partitioning Algorithm for Static Timing Analysis.
items.abstract: Recent static timing analysis (STA) engines have leveraged task dependency graph (TDG) parallelism to accelerate various STA algorithms, including graph-based analysis and path-based analysis. Despite the promising speedup via task parallelism, the scheduling cost of a TDG has become dominant when handling large TDGs. To overcome this challenge, we propose G-PASTA, a simple and fast TDG partitioning algorithm to reduce the scheduling cost of large task-parallel STA algorithms. By harnessing the power of GPU computing, G-PASTA incurs minimal cost of partitioning while bringing significant runtime improvement to task-parallel STA algorithms. Compared to a state-of-the-art CPU-based TDG partitioner, G-PASTA is up to 41.8× faster in partitioning runtime and can improve the overall STA performance by 43% on large designs.
items.abstract_zh: 最近的静态时序分析 (STA) 引擎利用任务依赖图 (TDG) 并行性来加速各种 STA 算法，包括基于图的分析和基于路径的分析。尽管通过任务并行性有望实现加速，但在处理大型 TDG 时，TDG 的调度成本已成为主导。为了克服这一挑战，我们提出了 G-PASTA，一种简单快速的 TDG 划分算法，以降低大型任务并行 STA 算法的调度成本。通过利用 GPU 计算的能力，G-PASTA 可以将分区成本降至最低，同时为任务并行 STA 算法带来显着的运行时改进。与最先进的基于 CPU 的 TDG 分区器相比，G-PASTA 的分区运行时间速度提高了 41.8 倍，并且可以将大型设计的整体 STA 性能提高 43%。

items.title: SpREM: Exploiting Hamming Sparsity for Fast Quantum Readout Error Mitigation.
SpectraFlux: Harnessing the Flow of Multi-FPGA in Mass Spectrometry Clustering.
items.abstract: The identification and quantification of proteins through mass spectrometry (MS) are foundational to proteomics, offering insights into biological systems and disease states. However, current clustering tools struggle to process large-scale datasets. We propose SpectraFlux, a multiple FPGA-based architecture for accelerated mass spectrum clustering that outperforms existing CPU, GPU, and FPGA designs. It employs heterogeneous clustering kernels for adaptive bucket size management and optimizes memory usage by distinguishing between on-chip and high-bandwidth memory (HBM) storage solutions. SpectraFlux is built upon the TAPA-CS framework, which automatically compiles and partitions a large dataflow design across multiple chips with RDMA-based inter-FPGA communication. Our solution shows a 2.7X speed up on a quad-FPGA platform compared to a single FPGA. Additionally, we introduce a refined cost model for frame-based inter-FPGA communication to better accommodate the variable data rates inherent in proteomic data processing. This reduces the inter-FPGA data movement by up to 73%. Finally, SpectraFlux achieves speedups of up to 11X and 17X over SOTA FPGA and GPU accelerators, respectively.
items.abstract_zh: 通过质谱 (MS) 鉴定和定量蛋白质是蛋白质组学的基础，可提供对生物系统和疾病状态的深入了解。然而，当前的聚类工具难以处理大规模数据集。我们提出了 SpectraFlux，这是一种基于多个 FPGA 的架构，用于加速质谱聚类，其性能优于现有的 CPU、GPU 和 FPGA 设计。它采用异构集群内核进行自适应存储桶大小管理，并通过区分片上和高带宽内存 (HBM) 存储解决方案来优化内存使用。 SpectraFlux 基于 TAPA-CS 框架构建，该框架通过基于 RDMA 的 FPGA 间通信自动编译和划分跨多个芯片的大型数据流设计。我们的解决方案显示，与单个 FPGA 相比，四核 FPGA 平台的速度提高了 2.7 倍。此外，我们还为基于帧的 FPGA 间通信引入了改进的成本模型，以更好地适应蛋白质组数据处理中固有的可变数据速率。这将 FPGA 间的数据移动减少了 73%。最后，SpectraFlux 比 SOTA FPGA 和 GPU 加速器分别实现高达 11 倍和 17 倍的加速。

items.title: EasyACIM: An End-to-End Automated Analog CIM with Synthesizable Architecture and Agile Design Space Exploration.
Efficient Memory Integration: MRAM-SRAM Hybrid Accelerator for Sparse On-Device Learning.
DH-TRNG: A Dynamic Hybrid TRNG with Ultra-High Throughput and Area-Energy Efficiency.
items.abstract: As a vital security primitive, the true random number generator (TRNG) is a mandatory component to build roots of trust for any encryption system. However, existing TRNGs suffer from bottlenecks of low throughput and high area-energy consumption. In this work, we propose DH-TRNG, a dynamic hybrid TRNG circuitry architecture with ultra-high throughput and area-energy efficiency. Our DH-TRNG exhibits portability to distinct process FPGAs and passes both NIST and AIS-31 tests without any post-processing. The experiments show it incurs only 8 slices with the highest throughput of 670Mbps and 620Mbps on Xilinx Virtex-6 and Artix-7, respectively. Compared to the state-of-the-art TRNGs, our proposed design has the highest Throughput/Slices Power with 2.63× increase.
items.abstract_zh: 作为重要的安全原语，真随机数生成器 (TRNG) 是为任何加密系统构建信任根的必备组件。然而，现有的TRNG存在吞吐量低和面积能耗高的瓶颈。在这项工作中，我们提出了 DH-TRNG，一种具有超高吞吐量和面积能效的动态混合 TRNG 电路架构。我们的 DH-TRNG 表现出对不同工艺 FPGA 的可移植性，并且无需任何后处理即可通过 NIST 和 AIS-31 测试。实验表明，它只需要 8 个片，在 Xilinx Virtex-6 和 Artix-7 上的最高吞吐量分别为 670Mbps 和 620Mbps。与最先进的 TRNG 相比，我们提出的设计具有最高的吞吐量/切片功率，增加了 2.63 倍。

items.title: Fracturing-aware Curvilinear ILT via Circular E-beam Mask Writer.
Disentangle, Align and Generalize: Learning A Timing Predictor from Different Technology Nodes.
A High-Performance Stochastic Simulated Bifurcation Ising Machine.
An Efficient Hardware Accelerator Design for Dynamic Graph Convolutional Network (DGCN) Inference.
A RRAM-based High Energy-efficient Accelerator Supporting Multimodal Tasks for Virtual Reality Wearable Devices.
items.abstract: Virtual reality (VR) wearable devices can achieve immersive entertainment by fusing multi-modal tasks from various senses. However, constrained by the short battery life and limited hardware resources of the VR devices, running multiple tasks simultaneously with different modals is difficult. In this paper, we propose an energy-efficient accelerator that supports Multi-modal Tasks for VR devices, namely MTVR. We present a multi-task computing solution based on the flexible multi-task computing core design and efficient computing unit allocation strategy, which simultaneously achieves efficient work of multi-modal tasks. We design an early exit detector to skip invalid calculations, greatly saving energy. In addition, a fine-grained tiny value skip method at multiplier and adder levels is proposed to save energy further. We provide a hybrid RRAM and SRAM memory access scheme, reducing the external memory access (EMA). Through experimental evaluation, the multitask computing core achieves an average computational utilization of 95%. When the invalid input ratio is 90%, energy saving brought by the early exit detector can reach 88%. The tiny value skip method further achieved 13% energy saving. Hybrid memory access scheme obtains 98.9% EMA reduction. We deployed the MTVR accelerator in FPGA and self-designed RRAM, achieving energy efficiency of 3.6 TOPS/W, higher than other single-task accelerators.
items.abstract_zh: 虚拟现实（VR）可穿戴设备可以通过融合多种感官的多模态任务来实现沉浸式娱乐。然而，受VR设备电池寿命短和硬件资源有限的限制，以不同模式同时运行多个任务是很困难的。在本文中，我们提出了一种支持 VR 设备多模式任务的节能加速器，即 MTVR。我们提出了一种基于灵活的多任务计算核心设计和高效的计算单元分配策略的多任务计算解决方案，同时实现了多模态任务的高效工作。我们设计了一个早期退出检测器来跳过无效计算，大大节省能源。此外，还提出了乘法器和加法器级别的细粒度微小值跳跃方法，以进一步节省能量。我们提供混合 RRAM 和 SRAM 存储器访问方案，减少外部存储器访问 (EMA)。通过实验评估，多任务计算核心的平均计算利用率达到95%。当无效输入比例为90%时，提前退出检测器带来的节能可达88%。微小值跳跃方法进一步实现了13%的节能。混合内存访问方案获得了 98.9% 的 EMA 降低。我们将MTVR加速器部署在FPGA和自行设计的RRAM中，实现了3.6 TOPS/W的能效，高于其他单任务加速器。

items.title: Neural Barrier Certificates Synthesis of NN-Controlled Continuous Systems via Counterexample-Guided Learning.
3D-Carbon: An Analytical Carbon Modeling Tool for 3D and 2.5D Integrated Circuits.
Multi-Resonance Mesh-Based Wavelength-Routed Optical Networks-on-Chip.
Improving the Efficiency of In-Memory-Computing Macro with a Hybrid Analog-Digital Computing Mode for Lossless Neural Network Inference.
EMOGen: Enhancing Mask Optimization via Pattern Generation.
CAMPER: Exploring the Potential of Content Addressable Memory for 3D Point Cloud Efficient Range Search.
Graph Neural Networks Automated Design and Deployment on Device-Edge Co-Inference Systems.
DNN-Defender: A Victim-Focused In-DRAM Defense Mechanism for Taming Adversarial Weight Attack on DNNs.
items.abstract: With deep learning deployed in many security-sensitive areas, machine learning security is becoming progressively important. Recent studies demonstrate attackers can exploit system-level techniques exploiting the RowHammer vulnerability of DRAM to deterministically and precisely flip bits in Deep Neural Networks (DNN) model weights to affect inference accuracy. The existing defense mechanisms are software-based, such as weight reconstruction requiring expensive training overhead or performance degradation. On the other hand, generic hardware-based victim-/aggressor-focused mechanisms impose expensive hardware overheads and preserve the spatial connection between victim and aggressor rows. In this paper, we present the first DRAM-based victim-focused defense mechanism tailored for quantized DNNs, named DNN-Defender that leverages the potential of in-DRAM swapping to withstand the targeted bit-flip attacks with a priority protection mechanism. Our results indicate that DNN-Defender can deliver a high level of protection downgrading the performance of targeted RowHammer attacks to a random attack level. In addition, the proposed defense has no accuracy drop on CIFAR-10 and ImageNet datasets without requiring any software training or incurring hardware overhead.
items.abstract_zh: 随着深度学习在许多安全敏感领域的部署，机器学习安全性变得越来越重要。最近的研究表明，攻击者可以利用系统级技术，利用 DRAM 的 RowHammer 漏洞来确定性地、精确地翻转深度神经网络 (DNN) 模型权重中的位，从而影响推理准确性。现有的防御机制是基于软件的，例如权重重建需要昂贵的训练开销或性能下降。另一方面，基于通用硬件的以受害者/攻击者为中心的机制会带来昂贵的硬件开销，并保留受害者和攻击者行之间的空间连接。在本文中，我们提出了第一个针对量化 DNN 量身定制的基于 DRAM 的以受害者为中心的防御机制，名为 DNN-Defender，它利用 DRAM 内交换的潜力，通过优先级保护机制来抵御有针对性的位翻转攻击。我们的结果表明，DNN-Defender 可以提供高水平的保护，将目标 RowHammer 攻击的性能降低到随机攻击级别。此外，所提出的防御方法在 CIFAR-10 和 ImageNet 数据集上的准确性没有下降，不需要任何软件训练或产生硬件开销。

items.title: LVF2: A Statistical Timing Model based on Gaussian Mixture for Yield Estimation and Speed Binning.
EnTurbo: Accelerate Confidential Serverless Computing via Parallelizing Enclave Startup Procedure.
items.abstract: Serverless computing has gained widespread attention, and Trusted Execution Environments (TEEs) are well-suited for safeguarding user privacy. However, the additional startup procedure introduced by TEEs imposes considerable performance overhead on confidential serverless workloads. This paper introduces a novel parallelized enclave startup design, EnTurbo, which eliminates the integrity dependence of the enclave startup procedure, accelerating it while ensuring its security. Additionally, EnTurbo parallelizes the measurement procedure, enabling multi-thread measurement for acceleration with provable security. We evaluate EnTurbo by running confidential serverless workloads on SGX simulation mode. Results show that EnTurbo effectively speeds up enclave serverless by 1.42x-6.48x (SGXv1) and 1.33x-3.76x (SGXv2).
items.abstract_zh: 无服务器计算受到广泛关注，可信执行环境（TEE）非常适合保护用户隐私。然而，TEE 引入的额外启动过程给机密无服务器工作负载带来了相当大的性能开销。本文介绍了一种新颖的并行Enclave启动设计EnTurbo，它消除了Enclave启动过程的完整性依赖，在加速的同时保证了其安全性。此外，EnTurbo 并行化了测量过程，支持多线程测量以实现加速，并具有可证明的安全性。我们通过在 SGX 模拟模式上运行机密无服务器工作负载来评估 EnTurbo。结果表明，EnTurbo 有效地将 enclave serverless 速度提高了 1.42x-6.48x (SGXv1) 和 1.33x-3.76x (SGXv2)。

items.title: ScaleFold: Reducing AlphaFold Initial Training Time to 10 Hours.
Late Breaking Results: Mixed-Cell-Height Detailed Placement under Multi-Cell Spacing Constraints.
Late Breaking Results: Evaluation of Human Action Quality with Linear Recurrent Units and Graph Attention Networks on Embedded Systems.
PONO: Power Optimization with Near Optimal SMT-based Sub-circuit Generation.
